meta.title: Hussien Essam · Frontend Developer
meta.description: Frontend developer crafting elegant interfaces for clients in Egypt, UAE, and Saudi Arabia.

phrases: I build product surfaces that feel calm under pressure.
I choreograph motion that earns its place.
I prototype fast, then ship slower, then better.
I work in English, Arabic, and a quiet third language called taste.
hero.lead: Hussien Essam — a frontend developer with six years building for startups and studios between Cairo, Dubai, and Riyadh. Bilingual by default, motion-first by habit.

about.title: A practice, not a pipeline.
about.p1: I'm Hussien, a frontend developer who treats detail as the deliverable. I work with product teams in Egypt, the UAE, and Saudi Arabia shipping product surfaces that have to earn their weight on launch day and still look composed a year later.
about.p2: My method is simple: decide the feeling first, write the interaction, then write the code. Whatever the framework, the rules still hold — restraint, rhythm, warmth.
about.quote: Detail is not decoration — it is the only promise a product can keep.

work.title: Builds that quietly do their job.
work.subtitle: Polished placeholder content — swap with your real shipped projects when ready.

cards.title: Khayal — Arabic typography studio site
cards.desc: A bilingual wordmark for a regional type foundry. Liquid headline type, variable-weight axis, RTL proof gallery.

cards.title: PlaySync — Point-of-Sale interface
cards.desc: Touch-first cashier surface with offline-first sync, Arabic-first typographic hierarchy, and quantity keys at 64px.

cards.title: Rawi — long-form reading app
cards.desc: Bilingual reader with adjustable Arabic kashida and a per-font baseline grid. Mag-style typography on dark.

capabilities.eyebrow: What I bring to a build
capabilities.title: Services, frankly described.
capabilities.subtitle: Pick a row to lean in. Each engagement is scoped as a fixed-shape engagement to keep momentum predictable.

items.title: Frontend engineering
items.desc: Production-grade React / Next / Vue with a real type system, a real test layer, and a real deploy pipeline.

items.title: Motion choreography
items.desc: GSAP and Framer timelines that earn their place — page transitions, scroll-driven reveals, custom cursors.

items.title: Arabic typography
items.desc: Variable font pairings, baseline grids tuned for Nastaliq and Naskh, kashida-aware readers.

items.title: Design systems
items.desc: Token-first systems with both Figma and code as source of truth, synced via Style Dictionary.

items.title: Performance audit
items.desc: Web Vitals investigation, hydration triage, image strategy, third-party tag surgery.
